title: Mortality rate, adult, male (per 1,000 male adults)
definition: Adult mortality rate, male, is the probability of dying between the ages of 15 and 60--that is, the probability of a 15-year-old male dying before reaching age 60, if subject to age-specific mortality rates of the specified year between those ages.

source.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International unless specifically labeled otherwise by World Bank metadata

editorial.shortHook: A mortality map with conflict, health, and risk-behavior clues.
editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include Lesotho, Central African Republic, Chad, while the lightest include United Arab Emirates, Qatar, Sweden; that contrast is the fastest way to read the male adult mortality pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Adult mortality exposes patterns that life expectancy can smooth away.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Female adult mortality can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Life expectancy can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Under-5 mortality can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.

editorial.difficultyReason: Expert difficulty: the map can be mistaken for adjacent indicators unless players probe a high, low, and middle country.
regionalSignals: Health maps often separate income, public-health capacity, and conflict exposure.
Check both high-income and low-income anchors.

reviewNotes: Distinctive enough for expert play because male risk patterns matter.
Too correlated with other mortality maps for default Daily.
